ux_issues.description: ListChatSessions returns messageCount: 0 for a session with 8+ messages exchanged. This may confuse the frontend when displaying session previews.
ux_issues.suggestion: Ensure messageCount is populated correctly from Firestore message count or tracked in-memory.

ux_issues.description: In turns 4 and 5, the AI called delete_itinerary_items before create_itinerary_items to reorganize the itinerary. While technically correct (it replaced earlier items with updated versions), the user never explicitly asked for deletions. Day 1 went from 2 items to 1 item between turns 5 and 6 final check, suggesting some items were silently removed during reorganization.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider notifying the user when items are deleted as part of itinerary reorganization rather than silently removing them.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: In Turn 3 (food experiences), the AI went beyond the request and proactively added days 8-14 (Machu Picchu through Lima return) even though only food experiences for Cusco were requested. This added 13 new items when the user only expected food additions to existing days.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Turn 3 was supposed to test whether the AI adds to existing days without wiping them. It did preserve existing items (count went from 20 to 33), but it also over-generated by building out the rest of the trip unsolicited.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: The AI called create_itinerary_items multiple times per turn with duplicate items, triggering the 'already_exists' deduplication guard. In turns 1 and 3, the second/third calls were fully skipped because all items already existed.
ai_behavior_issues.context: This wastes tool loop iterations (max 5) and API tokens. The AI should recognize from the first tool result that items were created and not re-submit the same list.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Excellent fabrication detection results: all 6 turns performed correctly with zero fabrication incidents. Per-turn item counts: Turn 1: 11 items, Turn 2: 20 items (+9), Turn 3: 33 items (+13), Turn 4: 42 items (+9), Turn 5: 45 items (+3), Turn 6: 45 items (no change, summary only). Final itinerary has 45 items across 14 days — well above the 15-item minimum. The AI reliably called create_itinerary_items on turns 1-5 and correctly refrained on turn 6 (summary). No fabrication phrases appeared without corresponding tool calls. The duplicate tool call issue (AI calling the same tool twice with identical items) is a minor inefficiency but does not affect correctness.
